heading: Annual appropriation
label: 322 Annual appropriation
text: There is annually appropriated, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, to be paid as provided in , to each State and Territory for the more complete endowment and maintenance of colleges for the benefit of agriculture and the mechanic arts established in accordance with the provisions of subchapter I of this chapter, $50,000 to be applied only to instruction in food and agricultural sciences, and to the facilities for such instruction: , That said colleges may use a portion of this money for providing courses for the special preparation of instructors for teaching the elements of food and agricultural sciences.section 324 of this titleProvided
notes: CodificationSection is based on a part of section 1 of act , and the tenth and eleventh pars. under the heading “Emergency Appropriations” of act . Remainder of section 1 of act , is classified to .Aug. 30, 1890Mar. 4, 1907Aug. 30, 1890section 323 of this title

Amendments1981— substituted “food and agricultural sciences” for “agriculture, the mechanic arts, the English language, and the various branches of mathematical, physical, natural, and economic science, with special reference to their applications in the industries of life” and “the elements of food and agricultural sciences” for “the elements of agriculture and the mechanic arts”.Pub. L. 97–981907—Act , substituted “$50,000” for “$25,000”, and inserted proviso.Mar. 4, 1907
Effective Date of 1981 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 97–98section 1801 of Pub. L. 97–98section 4301 of this titleDec. 22, 1981
Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions under this section to Secretary of Agriculture, see note set out under .section 321 of this title
